questions.question: Solve for x: |x - 5| = 12
questions.explanation: The absolute value equation splits into two cases: x - 5 = 12 (giving x = 17) and x - 5 = -12 (giving x = -7). Check both: |17 - 5| = 12 ✓ and |-7 - 5| = 12 ✓

questions.question: Solve for all x: |2x + 3| < 7
questions.explanation: Absolute value inequality |A| < B becomes -B < A < B. So -7 < 2x + 3 < 7. Subtract 3: -10 < 2x < 4. Divide by 2: -5 < x < 2.

questions.question: A thermometer's reading is guaranteed to be within 0.5°C of the actual temperature. If the thermometer reads 22°C, write an absolute value inequality for the actual temperature T.
questions.explanation: The distance between actual temperature T and the reading 22 must be at most 0.5°C. This is expressed as |T - 22| ≤ 0.5, or equivalently 21.5 ≤ T ≤ 22.5.

questions.question: Solve for x: |3x - 4| = |x + 8|
questions.explanation: When two absolute values are equal, either 3x - 4 = x + 8 or 3x - 4 = -(x + 8). First case: 2x = 12, so x = 6. Second case: 3x - 4 = -x - 8, so 4x = -4, giving x = -2.

questions.question: Solve for x: |x + 2| ≥ 9
questions.explanation: The absolute value inequality |A| ≥ B becomes A ≤ -B or A ≥ B. So x + 2 ≤ -9 or x + 2 ≥ 9. This gives x ≤ -11 or x ≥ 7.

questions.question: A production line manufactures bolts. The diameter must satisfy |d - 10| ≤ 0.2 millimeters, where d is the actual diameter. What is the maximum allowable diameter?
questions.explanation: Solve |d - 10| ≤ 0.2 by writing -0.2 ≤ d - 10 ≤ 0.2. Add 10 to all parts: 9.8 ≤ d ≤ 10.2. The maximum allowable diameter is 10.2 mm.
